zh.lifeSummary: 當Monte Lyman Cunningham於1876年12月4日出生在美國愛阿華州沃斯諾武德時，他的父親Martin Cunningham48歲，母親Caroline Wheelock42歲。他於1900年11月30日與Sarah S Reightley在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特帕克 拉皮茲結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特可洛 文 蕾克 堂史普住了大約30年且於1950年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特耐維斯號 堂史普。他去世於1952年9月30日在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特享年75歲，埋葬在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特巴度拉 堂史普萊蒙 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Cunningham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the province of Cunningham in Ayrshire, first recorded in 1153 in the form Cunegan, a Celtic name of uncertain origin. The spellings in -ham, first recorded in 1180, and in -ynghame, first recorded in 1227, represent a gradual assimilation to the English placename element -ingham.

Irish: surname adopted from Gaelic Ó Cuinneagáin ‘descendant of Cuinneagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of the Old Irish personal name Conn meaning ‘leader, chief’. This name is also adopted for Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin ‘descendant of Connagán’, from a diminutive of the personal name Conn.
History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Wernebald de Cunynghame, who was granted the manor of Cunningham by Hugh de Morville in the early 12th century.

variants.name: Cunigan
meanings: Altered form of Scottish or Irish   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Conahan
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Connachaín (see Cunningham ).

variants.name: Conaghan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Fergal.
Scottish (Lanarkshire and Renfrewshire) Irish (Donegal): from Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin, see Cunningham .

variants.name: Cuningham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cummingham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cunnigham
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): variant of   Cunningham  .

nameMeanings.name: Monte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Montgomery   or of the much rarer   Montague   and   Montmorency .  It is now also found as an independent given name. As a Jewish name, it was originally used as an approximate English equivalent of   Moses .

nameMeanings.name: Lyman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname representing a diminutive of lomm ‘bare’; borne by various early Irish saints, including a nephew of St Patrick who became first bishop of Trim in Meath in the early 5th century.

variants.name: Loman
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname representing a diminutive of lomm ‘bare’; borne by various early Irish saints, including a nephew of St Patrick who became first bishop of Trim in Meath in the early 5th century.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.
Historical Boundaries: 1883: Hubbard, Minnesota, United States

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: The Kensington Runestone
historicEvents.description: A Swedish man, Olof Ohman, was farming on his land when he came across a 202-pound rock slab that had strange writing on it. Convinced it was proof that Scandinavian explorers came to that area before Columbus found the Americas, he had it looked at by scholars and linguists to find its translation. There has been a drawn-out debate on the stone's authenticity, with a scholarly consensus that classifies it as a hoax and the community which is convinced that it is authentic.

historicEvents.title: Home of the Federal Reserve Bank for the Ninth District
historicEvents.description: The first building for the Federal Reserve bank in Minneapolis was completed in 1915 and was a peculiar structure. It had no windows on the lower walls close to the street and later, a small skyscraper was added to the top. It was created to serve the states of Montana, North and South Dakota, Minnesota, and the northern parts of Wisconsin and Michigan. Even though it covers a wide area, it serves the smallest population base of the entire reserve system. Today the Federal Reserve is housed in three buildings that are housed a few blocks away from each other.

historicEvents.title: The First woman elected into the US Congress
historicEvents.description: Jeannette Pickering Rankin became the first woman to hold a federal office position in the House of Representatives, and remains the only woman elected to Congress by Montana.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The First woman elected into the US Congress

historicEvents.title: The Grout Museum
historicEvents.description: The museum started as a private collection given to the city by Henry W. Grout. Today it is still a nonprofit educational museum that helps engage students and all people from the surrounding communities.

historicEvents.title: The FBI is Established
historicEvents.description: The Bureau of Investigation's name was changed to the Federal Bureau of Investigation to help citizens know that the Government is helping protect from threats both domestically and abroad.

en.lifeSummary: When Monte Lyman Cunningham was born on 4 December 1876, in Northwood, Worth, Iowa, United States, his father, Martin Cunningham, was 48 and his mother, Caroline Wheelock, was 42. He married Sarah S Reightley on 30 November 1900, in Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He lived in Crow Wing Lake Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States for about 30 years and Nevis Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1950. He died on 30 September 1952, in Hubbard, Minnesota, United States, at the age of 75, and was buried in Lemon Cemetery, Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Monte Lyman Cunningham(이)가 1876년 12월 4일 미국 아이오와 주 워스 노르스우드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Martin Cunningham(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Caroline Wheelock(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1900년 11월 30일 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 박 라피드즈에서 Sarah S Reightley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 크로 윙 레이크 타운시프에서 약 30년, 1950년도에 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 네비스 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1952년 9월 30일 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 바다라 타운시프 레몬 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Monte Lyman Cunningham родился 4 декабря 1876 г. в местности нортвуд, ворт, Айова, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Martin Cunningham было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Caroline Wheelock, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah S Reightley 30 ноября 1900 г. в местности парк рапидс, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он жил в местности кроу виг леик тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты около 30 лет и в местности невис тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1950 году. Он умер 30 сентября 1952 г. в местности хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности лемон кеметири, бадоура тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Monte Lyman Cunningham nació el 4 de diciembre de 1876, en Northwood, Worth, Iowa, Estados Unidos, su papá, Martin Cunningham, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Caroline Wheelock, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Sarah S Reightley el 30 de noviembre de 1900, en Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minnesota, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Crow Wing Lake Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, Estados Unidos durante unos 30 años y en Nevis Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, Estados Unidos en 1950. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1952, en Hubbard, Minnesota, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Lemon Cemetery, Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, Estados Unidos.
